source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division — Frank and Frances Carpenter Collection
artist: Unknown photographer (Frank and Frances Carpenter Collection, LoC)
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: Salmon cannery about twenty miles south of Ketchikan, in the Tongass National Forest
description: Circa 1900 photograph of a Southeast Alaska salmon cannery on pilings above the water about 20 miles south of Ketchikan inside the Tongass National Forest. Workers stand on the wharf with stacks of salmon barrels, with forested mountains rising directly behind the cannery. Commercial salmon canning was the dominant industry along the Tongass coast for most of the 20th century and is still a major presence today.
alt: 1900 black-and-white photograph of a Tongass National Forest salmon cannery on pilings with stacked barrels and forested mountains behind
